description: This figure presents room-temperature engineering stress-strain curves of Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si medium-manganese low-density steels subjected to cold rolling, annealing at three different temperatures (750℃, 800℃, 850℃) for 30 min followed by air cooling, then tempering at 400℃ for 30 min with air cooling. The x-axis is engineering strain in %, and the y-axis is engineering stress in MPa. The gray curve corresponds to the 750℃ annealed sample (ANT750), the red curve to the 800℃ annealed sample (ANT800), and the blue curve to the 850℃ annealed sample (ANT850). It is observed that as annealing temperature increases, the ultimate tensile strength rises from ~1070 MPa (750℃) to ~1230 MPa (800℃) and ~1300 MPa (850℃), while the elongation to failure decreases significantly from ~34% to ~13% and ~9%, respectively. The tested yield strength values of the three samples are 709 MPa, 968 MPa, and 964 MPa, respectively, which are used to validate the developed multi-mechanism strengthening based yield strength prediction model, with an average prediction error of only 1.56%.
